articles.title: Blistering Heat in Nagpur as Mercury Records to 43.6°C - The Live Nagpur
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Maharashtra
articles.full_text:
Vidarbha continued to reel under intense heat on Thursday, with Chandrapur emerging as the hottest city in the region at 44.6°C, according to the latest weather data. Amravati recorded 44.0°C, while Nagpur touched 43.6°C. Akola, Wardha and Gadchiroli also witnessed severe heat, each recording a maximum temperature of 43.6°C.
Brahmapuri registered 43.2°C, Gondia recorded 42.2°C, Bhandara 42.0°C, Yavatmal 41.8°C and Buldhana 41.0°C. Most districts continued to experience dry weather conditions with no rainfall reported during the last 24 hours. Humidity levels remained moderate across the region, adding to the discomfort faced by residents.
The scorching temperatures have significantly affected daily life, with people avoiding outdoor movement during afternoon hours. Health experts have advised citizens to stay hydrated, avoid direct exposure to sunlight and take necessary precautions as the heatwave conditions are likely to persist across Vidarbha over the next few days.
People to drink plenty of water, wear light cotton clothes and avoid stepping out between 12 noon and 4 pm unless necessary. Citizens have also been advised to consume fresh fruits and fluids, use umbrellas or caps while travelling outdoors and immediately seek medical help in case of dizziness, dehydration or heatstroke symptoms.
